text.title: QA Test Data Catalog Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the qa-test-data-catalog methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Every fixture has a stable string id (kebab-case, scoped by service). Tests reference by id, never inline.
rule.rationale: Inline fixtures drift; named ids let the catalog be the single source of truth.

rule.statement: Each fixture is flagged contains_pii ∈ {true, false}. PII fixtures may only contain synthesised or anonymised data.
rule.rationale: Without PII flagging, real personal data leaks into test envs; with flag, audit is automatic.

rule.statement: Each fixture declares refresh ∈ {test, function, module, session, persistent}. Persistent fixtures live in a seed dump and are refreshed manually.
rule.rationale: Without refresh declaration, state leaks across tests and causes order-dependent flakes.

rule.statement: Each fixture names the owning service/app responsible for its shape (e.g. orders, payments).
rule.rationale: Cross-service ownership disperses responsibility; one owner = one keeper.

rule.statement: CI fails when tests create model instances with raw ORM calls instead of catalog factories (lint rule).
rule.rationale: Soft enforcement decays in weeks; lint enforces.

rule.statement: Catalog lists consumers (test files) of each fixture. Stale fixtures (zero consumers > 60d) are flagged for removal.
rule.rationale: Untracked fixtures accumulate dead data; tracked ones can be pruned.

rule.statement: If suite <50 tests OR project pre-MVP OR fully hermetic from primitives, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Catalog ceremony is wasted in those cases.
